title: Lasiocephalus heterophyllus
taxonomy: Lasiocephalus heterophyllus is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Compositae, commonly known as the aster or daisy family. This plant is native to the Americas and is often found in mountainous regions. It is classified as a dicotyledon, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seeds. Lasiocephalus heterophyllus is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of plants that are closely related to the genus Senecio. The exact classification of this plant is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to revision in the future.
